content.text:
<user_query>
front end deployment failed chore: forensic reconciliation of remaining calibration chat commits … #231
</user_query>
I’m going to quickly inspect the current repo/terminal state to identify what failed in frontend deployment and map it to the `#231` chore context before making any changes.

I found prior terminal output referencing large calibration chat changes, but not yet the specific frontend deployment error. Next I’ll inspect this worktree’s git history and CI/deploy config to pinpoint what `#231` needs reconciled.
I’m now checking the CI/deploy workflow definitions to identify the exact frontend deploy failure condition and what commit reconciliation is still missing.
I couldn’t find that chore text in the codebase, so I’m pulling issue/PR `#231` details from GitHub to align the fix with the exact forensic reconciliation request.

I’ve confirmed there isn’t an existing `#231` artifact in GitHub yet, so I’m now isolating the likely frontend build/deploy break in this branch (especially Docker + npm lockfile consistency), then I’ll patch and verify.

I now have the root cause from CI logs: multiple frontend files contain merge-artifact syntax issues from the calibration-chat reconciliation commit. I’m going to open and repair those specific files, then run a Node 20 Docker build to validate the deploy path.

I found the key divergence: your local `main` is behind `origin/main`, and `origin/main` now points to the failing reconciliation commit. I’m comparing that remote commit against your current local state to reconcile only the broken frontend pieces.

I’m on top of the failing `origin/main` commit now, and confirmed duplicate declarations exist in the affected frontend files. I’m restoring the known-good versions of those exact files from your last stable local commit and then running a containerized frontend build check.

Frontend deploy failure is reproducible to the failed `origin/main` run titled `chore: forensic reconciliation of remaining calibration chat commits (#198)`.

Root cause was merge-reconciliation fallout in frontend files (duplicate declarations / malformed JS), exactly matching the failed CI errors from `Build Frontend -> Build & push frontend image`:
- `ChiefMarketingSummary.js`: duplicate identifier (`impact`)
- `WebsiteLayout.js`: duplicate identifier (`MEET_BIQC_ITEMS`)
- `SupabaseAuthContext.js`: syntax break around line ~586
- `useCalibrationState.js`: non-top-level import/export parse failure
- `api.js`: duplicate `pathname` declaration
- `CalibrationQaAccess.js`: non-top-level import/export parse failure
- `MarketPage.js`: duplicate identifier (`d`)
- `HomePage.js`: duplicate import (`ModernIntegrationBanner`)

What I did:
- Created branch `fix/frontend-deploy-reconcile` from `origin/main`.
- Restored those 8 broken frontend files to the known-good versions from commit `814dd7ae` (your local stable calibration port commit).
- Confirmed staged files now exactly match `814dd7ae` for those paths.
- Preserved your pre-existing `package-lock.json` local change as unstaged (not included in this frontend fix).

Current git state:
- **Staged:** the 8 frontend reconciliation fixes (ready to commit)
- **Unstaged:** `package-lock.json` (your local existing change)

I also pulled the failed Actions metadata and verified the exact failure step and error signatures before applying this reconciliation.

If you want, I can now:
1) create the commit for these staged fixes, and  
2) push `fix/frontend-deploy-reconcile` and open a PR so you can rerun deployment cleanly.
Proceeding with the publish flow now: I’ll validate staged changes, create the fix commit, push the branch, and open a PR against `main`.
I hit shell-quoting issues with heredoc on this Windows PowerShell session; I’m finishing the commit with the same message content using standard `git commit -m` so we can proceed with push/PR now.

Done end-to-end.

- Committed the frontend reconciliation fix on branch `fix/frontend-deploy-reconcile`:
  - `fd506bac` — `fix: restore frontend calibration modules after forensic merge`
- Pushed branch to remote and opened PR:
  - [https://github.com/andrealexopoulos-star/Version2-Ai_Mentor/pull/200](https://github.com/andrealexopoulos-star/Version2-Ai_Mentor/pull/200)
- Left your unrelated local change untouched:
  - `package-lock.json` is still modified locally and **not** included in the commit/PR.

If you want, I can also watch the PR checks and report back once the `Build Frontend` job passes/fails.
